events.title: Speaker Pelosi Arrives in Taipei
events.detail: US House Speaker Nancy Pelosi lands in Taipei on August 2, 2022, becoming the highest-ranking US official to visit Taiwan since Speaker Newt Gingrich in 1997. The visit defies intense PRC warnings and triggers the most severe cross-strait military crisis since 1996. PLA aircraft immediately begin crossing the Taiwan Strait median line in unprecedented numbers.

media.source: Wikipedia / 2022 visit by Nancy Pelosi to Taiwan

events.title: PLA Announces Six Exercise Zones Encircling Taiwan
events.detail: China's PLA Eastern Theater Command announces six live-fire exercise zones surrounding Taiwan, covering waters northeast, east, southeast, south, southwest, and west of the island. The zones overlap with Taiwan's territorial waters and major shipping lanes. The exercises, scheduled August 4–7, represent the largest PLA military maneuvers around Taiwan in decades, exceeding the 1996 Third Taiwan Strait Crisis in scope.

events.title: PLA Fires 11 Ballistic Missiles Over and Around Taiwan
events.detail: Between August 4–9, 2022, the PLA fires 11 Dongfeng-series ballistic missiles during exercises. Five land in Japan's EEZ, prompting a formal protest from Tokyo. This marks the first PLA ballistic missile overflights of Taiwan's main island and double the number fired in the 1995–96 Taiwan Strait crises. The missiles arc over Taiwan's northern coastline at approximately 25°N.

events.title: PLA Systematically Erases Taiwan Strait Median Line
events.detail: During August 4–10, more than 250 PLA aircraft sorties cross the Taiwan Strait median line — a tacit boundary observed since the 1950s. PLA spokespeople explicitly deny the median line's existence, declaring the Taiwan Strait to be China's internal waters. This 'new normal' of continuous median-line crossings persists for the remainder of 2022 and beyond, fundamentally altering the strategic calculus.

events.title: China Extends Exercises to Full Week; 7th Zone Added
events.detail: China extends its initial 4-day exercise to a full week (August 4–11), adding a 7th exercise zone. Drills include simulated blockade operations, anti-submarine warfare, and amphibious assault rehearsals. US Navy carrier USS Ronald Reagan repositions to the Philippine Sea east of Taiwan rather than transiting the strait, signaling concern about escalation.

events.title: Japan Formally Protests PLA Missiles Landing in Its EEZ
events.detail: Five PLA ballistic missiles land inside Japan's Exclusive Economic Zone southwest of Okinawa. Japan's Ministry of Defense publicly identifies and protests the incursion — the first time PLA exercises have directly impacted Japanese sovereign maritime space. Prime Minister Fumio Kishida calls it 'a serious problem affecting Japan's national security.' The episode galvanizes Japanese public support for accelerated defense spending.

events.title: PLA Announces Early Exercise Conclusion; Commits to Regular Patrols
events.detail: On August 10, China's PLA Eastern Theater Command announces an early conclusion to the main exercises but immediately commits to 'regular patrols and combat readiness exercises' in the Taiwan Strait. The August 2022 monthly total reaches 446 ADIZ incursions — an all-time record — with 302 median-line crossings in a single month (24× the pre-Pelosi baseline).

events.title: USS Ronald Reagan CSG Repositions to Philippine Sea
events.detail: The USS Ronald Reagan carrier strike group (CSG) repositions from the South China Sea to the Philippine Sea during PLA exercises, maintaining a deterrent presence east of Taiwan without directly entering the strait. The US also deploys additional P-8 Poseidon maritime patrol aircraft to the region to monitor PLA naval movements. Biden administration maintains open channels with Beijing to prevent miscalculation.

events.title: PLA Establishes 'New Normal' of Continuous Median-Line Crossings
events.detail: Following the August exercises, PLA aircraft continue crossing the Taiwan Strait median line on an almost daily basis. From September through December 2022, an additional 261 PLA aircraft cross the median line. By year-end 2022, the annual ADIZ incursion total reaches 1,737 — a 79% increase from 2021 — and 564 median-line crossings are recorded (a 24× increase from pre-Pelosi norms). The Taiwan Strait's tacit boundary effectively ceases to exist as an operational constraint.

events.title: USS Higgins and USS Antietam Transit Taiwan Strait
events.detail: In October 2022, the USS Higgins (destroyer) and USS Antietam (cruiser) conduct a routine Taiwan Strait transit, the first dual-ship transit since the Pelosi crisis exercises. PLA naval vessels shadow the transit at close range. The US Indo-Pacific Command describes it as 'consistent with international law, demonstrating the US commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.' Beijing protests the transit as 'provocation.'

events.title: Biden-Xi Summit at G20 Bali: Taiwan Tensions Discussed
events.detail: US President Joe Biden and Chinese President Xi Jinping hold their first in-person summit on the sidelines of the G20 in Bali, Indonesia on November 14, 2022. Biden tells Xi the US 'strongly opposes unilateral efforts to change the status quo' on Taiwan and reaffirms the one-China policy. Xi warns that Taiwan is 'the first red line' in US-China relations. Both sides agree to maintain open military communication channels to prevent accidents.

events.title: Taiwan Local Elections: DPP Suffers Significant Setbacks
events.detail: In Taiwan's November 26, 2022 local elections, the ruling Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) suffers its worst local election result in years, losing major municipalities including Taoyuan, Chiayi, and Keelung. The KMT (Kuomintang) gains ground, reflecting public fatigue and economic concerns. President Tsai Ing-wen resigns as DPP party chairperson but retains the presidency. The results complicate cross-strait policymaking ahead of the 2024 presidential race.

events.title: US NDAA 2023 Includes Major Taiwan Defense Provisions
events.detail: The US National Defense Authorization Act for FY2023, signed December 23, 2022, includes landmark Taiwan security provisions: authorization of up to $10 billion in foreign military financing for Taiwan over five years, streamlined arms sale processes, and provisions for expanded US-Taiwan military training exchanges. The law represents the most substantial US legislative support for Taiwan's defense since the 1979 Taiwan Relations Act.

events.title: PLA Drones Begin Systematic Surveillance of Taiwan's Offshore Islands
events.detail: In autumn 2022, PLA unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) begin conducting increasingly close reconnaissance flights around Taiwan's Kinmen, Matsu, and Wuqiu offshore islands — located just miles from mainland China's Fujian coast. Taiwan's military scrambles jets to intercept the drones but faces difficult rules of engagement given the proximity. By year-end, drone incidents near offshore islands become a near-weekly occurrence.

events.title: Tsai Meets US House Speaker McCarthy at Reagan Library
events.detail: On April 5, 2023, Taiwan President Tsai Ing-wen meets US House Speaker Kevin McCarthy at the Ronald Reagan Presidential Library in Simi Valley, California. This is the first meeting between a Taiwan president and a US House Speaker on US soil since 1979. McCarthy was accompanied by a bipartisan delegation of 18 members of Congress. Beijing condemns the meeting as a 'serious provocation' and announces military exercises in response.

events.title: PLA Launches 'Joint Sword-2023' Exercise in Response to McCarthy Meeting
events.detail: Following the Tsai-McCarthy meeting, China's PLA Eastern Theater Command launches 'Joint Sword-2023' on April 8-10, 2023. The exercise involves multi-domain operations simulating encirclement of Taiwan with strikes on key infrastructure targets. Dozens of PLA aircraft and warships participate. The drills simulate precision strikes, sea control, airspace control, and information dominance operations — representing a significant evolution from the August 2022 exercises.

events.title: US Deploys Additional F-35 and F-22 Squadrons to Kadena Air Base
events.detail: In 2023, the US Air Force dramatically expands its 5th-generation fighter presence at Kadena Air Base in Okinawa, deploying additional F-35 Lightning II and F-22 Raptor squadrons on rotational basis. This follows the retirement of the permanently-based F-15C/D fleet. Kadena, located approximately 650 km from China's coastline, serves as the primary US forward air operations hub for any Taiwan contingency.

events.title: Taiwan Strait Median Line Crossed on 271 Days in 2023
events.detail: Full-year 2023 statistics show the PLA crossed the Taiwan Strait centerline on 271 days — approximately 74% of the year. This represents the normalization of what was previously a highly unusual occurrence. Taiwan's MND notes that PLA presence now includes reconnaissance aircraft, fighter jets, bombers, and drones conducting coordinated multi-type operations, reflecting a more mature and sophisticated pressure campaign.

events.title: TSMC Arizona Fab Opens; US CHIPS Act Reshapes Semiconductor Geography
events.detail: In December 2022 (groundbreaking) and into 2023, TSMC's first Arizona fabrication plant begins construction with US CHIPS Act subsidies of approximately $6.6 billion. While the Arizona fab will produce 4nm chips — significantly behind Taiwan's cutting-edge processes — it marks a US effort to reduce dependence on Taiwan for semiconductor manufacturing. TSMC still controls ~70% of global foundry output and ~92% of advanced logic chip manufacturing, maintaining Taiwan's strategic semiconductor position.

events.title: US Approves $619 Million Arms Sale to Taiwan: F-16 Munitions
events.detail: On March 28, 2023, the US Defense Security Cooperation Agency (DSCA) notifies Congress of a $619 million arms sale to Taiwan, comprising AIM-9X Sidewinder missiles, AIM-120 AMRAAMs, and associated F-16 fighter jet equipment. Beijing formally protests, calling it 'interference in China's internal affairs.' The sale is part of a growing US effort to enhance Taiwan's air-to-air combat capability against numerically superior PLA air forces.

events.title: Japan Announces Historic Defense Budget Doubling to 2% GDP
events.detail: Japan announces a historic doubling of its defense budget to 2% of GDP by 2027, citing the deteriorating security environment in the Taiwan Strait and North Korean missile threats. The plan, totaling approximately $315 billion over five years, includes acquisition of Tomahawk cruise missiles with range to strike Chinese mainland bases, Aegis Ashore missile defense systems, and F-35B short-takeoff/vertical-landing aircraft. Japanese planners explicitly identify a Taiwan contingency as a top threat scenario.

events.title: China Unilaterally Changes Civilian Aviation Routes Near Median Line
events.detail: On January 30, 2024, China's CAAC unilaterally activates revised aviation routes M503, W122, and W123 that bring civilian aircraft closer to the Taiwan Strait median line without consulting Taiwan. Taiwan and the US protest the unilateral action, calling it a destabilizing erosion of existing tacit agreements. The route changes signal Beijing's continued rejection of the median line as a meaningful boundary.

events.title: Lai Ching-te Wins Taiwan Presidential Election
events.detail: On January 13, 2024, William Lai Ching-te (DPP) wins Taiwan's presidential election with approximately 40.1% of the popular vote in a three-way race, defeating KMT's Hou Yu-ih (33.5%) and TPP's Ko Wen-je (26.5%). The DPP loses its legislative majority, creating a divided government. Beijing immediately denounces Lai as a 'dangerous separatist' and 'troublemaker' threatening peace and stability. Lai takes office May 20, 2024.

events.title: Nauru Switches Diplomatic Recognition to PRC
events.detail: On January 15, 2024 — just two days after Taiwan's election — the Pacific island nation of Nauru switches its diplomatic recognition from Taiwan to the People's Republic of China. The timing is widely viewed as Beijing's calculated response to Lai's election victory. Taiwan's formal diplomatic partners drop to 12 countries. Taiwan's foreign minister calls it a 'political manipulation' by Beijing.

events.title: Lai Ching-te Inaugurated as Taiwan's 8th President
events.detail: On May 20, 2024, Lai Ching-te is inaugurated as Taiwan's 8th President and 16th-term ROC President, with Hsiao Bi-khim as Vice President. In his inaugural address, Lai reaffirms Taiwan's sovereignty, calls on China to 'face reality,' and says Taiwan will neither bow to pressure nor provoke conflict. Beijing's Taiwan Affairs Office brands the speech 'separatist,' saying it 'exposes Lai's true face as a troublemaker for cross-strait peace.'

events.title: PLA Launches 'Joint Sword-2024A': Largest Naval Deployment to Date
events.detail: On May 23–24, 2024, just days after Lai's inauguration, China's PLA launches 'Joint Sword-2024A' exercise. 46 naval vessels deploy around Taiwan — the largest PLA naval mobilization ever recorded in the Taiwan Strait. The exercise covers all sides of Taiwan including the Kinmen, Matsu, Wuqiu, and Dongyin offshore islands. Operations include simulated precision strikes, sea control, and anti-submarine warfare. The Eastern, Southern, and Northern Theater Commands all participate.

events.title: PLA Sets Record: 313 Days with Median Line Crossings in 2024
events.detail: Full-year 2024 statistics show PLA aircraft and naval vessels crossed the Taiwan Strait centerline on 313 days — nearly 86% of all days. The annual sortie count reaches approximately 3,070 — nearly double 2023 figures. Analysts at CSIS and the Global Taiwan Institute note that PLA operations have evolved from episodic provocations to sustained, systematic pressure designed to normalize Chinese presence in Taiwan's vicinity.

events.title: 'Joint Sword-2024B' Exercise: Blockade Scenario Focus
events.detail: On October 14, 2024, China launches 'Joint Sword-2024B,' a more focused exercise emphasizing blockade/containment scenarios rather than amphibious invasion. The exercise involves PLA Navy surface combatants, coast guard vessels, and air assets conducting containment drills in seven designated operation zones. The inclusion of China Coast Guard (CCG) vessels alongside PLAN marks a significant escalation, blurring military and law enforcement roles to complicate Taiwan's legal response.

events.title: Donald Trump Wins US Presidential Election; Taiwan Policy Shifts
events.detail: Donald Trump wins the November 5, 2024 US presidential election, defeating incumbent Vice President Kamala Harris. Trump's return to the White House raises significant questions about US commitment to Taiwan: he has criticized Taiwan's semiconductor dominance ('took 100% of our chip business'), suggested Taiwan should pay more for US defense, and historically shown preference for 'strategic ambiguity.' Nevertheless, his administration ultimately approves the largest-ever arms sale to Taiwan in December 2025.

events.title: Taiwan Approves $40 Billion 8-Year Special Defense Budget
events.detail: Taiwan's Legislative Yuan approves a special supplemental defense budget of approximately $40 billion USD to be spent over 8 years on indigenous weapons development, submarine construction, missile systems, and military training. The budget is separate from the regular annual defense appropriation and signals Lai's commitment to reaching 3.3% GDP defense spending in 2026 and a 5% goal by 2030. The DPP's legislative minority requires coalition support to pass the measure.

events.title: Trump Returns to Strategic Ambiguity on Taiwan Defense Commitment
events.detail: After taking office on January 20, 2025, President Trump reverses Biden's explicit Taiwan defense commitments, returning to 'strategic ambiguity.' When asked whether he would defend Taiwan militarily, Trump responds: 'I never comment on that. I don't want to ever put myself in that position.' Trump also publicly demands Taiwan increase defense spending from 2.5% to 10% of GDP, calling Taiwan's level 'ridiculously low.' Taiwan officials publicly express concern about the policy shift.

events.title: 'Strait Thunder-2025A': First Missile Fires into Strait Since 2022
events.detail: In April 2025, China's PLA conducts 'Strait Thunder-2025A,' marking the first PLA ballistic or rocket artillery fires into the Taiwan Strait since the August 2022 Pelosi crisis exercises. Rockets land near Taiwan's 24-nautical-mile contiguous zone boundary. Taiwan's MND calls the exercise 'an escalatory provocation.' The drills involve maritime patrol, surface warfare, and electronic warfare components alongside the rocket artillery component.

events.title: Taiwan Sets 2026 Defense Budget at $31 Billion (3.3% GDP)
events.detail: In response to US pressure and growing PLA threat, Taiwan's Lai administration proposes a 2026 defense budget of approximately $31 billion USD — equivalent to 3.3% of GDP, the highest peacetime level in Taiwan's history. The budget prioritizes asymmetric warfare capabilities: mobile missile launchers, drone swarms, coastal defense systems, and reserve force training. Taiwan's opposition KMT questions whether the spending increase is affordable without tax reform.

events.title: Trump Approves Record $11.15 Billion Arms Sale to Taiwan
events.detail: On December 18, 2025, the Trump administration notifies Congress of an $11.154 billion arms sale to Taiwan — the largest single US arms package to Taiwan in history. The package includes HIMARS rocket systems, M109A6 Paladin howitzers, drones, anti-armor munitions, and associated equipment. Despite Trump's rhetorical skepticism about Taiwan, the sale demonstrates continued bipartisan institutional support for Taiwan's defense. Beijing condemns the sale as 'a grave violation of China's sovereignty.'

events.title: 'Justice Mission-2025': Largest PLA Maritime Deployment on Record
events.detail: On December 29–30, 2025, the PLA launches 'Justice Mission-2025' in retaliation for the US arms sale. The exercise becomes the largest PLA maritime deployment ever recorded: 130 aircraft sorties, 90 of which cross the Taiwan Strait centerline; 14 PLAN warships plus 11 coast guard vessels operate in Taiwan's contiguous zone. Seven designated operation zones encompass Taiwan on all sides. Analysts identify the exercise as a blockade rehearsal emphasizing coast guard enforcement — suggesting a legal/gray-zone blockade strategy rather than kinetic warfare.

events.title: PLA Conducts Joint Combat Readiness Drill 40 nm off Taiwan's West Coast
events.detail: On February 26, 2025, China's PLA Eastern Theater Command conducts a joint combat readiness drill approximately 40 nautical miles off Taiwan's southwest and western coasts. The drill includes fighter jets, reconnaissance UAVs, and surface warships operating in a coordinated multi-domain formation. Taiwan's MND issues a protest and activates air defense systems. The exercise is notable for occurring outside any formal 'exercise' announcement period — suggesting it is now simply routine operations.

events.title: TSMC Achieves Record 70.2% Global Foundry Share in Q2 2025
events.detail: TSMC reports its global semiconductor foundry market share rises to a record 70.2% in Q2 2025, up from approximately 57% in 2021. Revenue exceeds $30 billion per quarter driven by AI chip demand from NVIDIA, Apple, and AMD. TSMC's leading-edge 3nm and 2nm processes remain exclusively manufactured in Taiwan, reinforcing the island's status as the 'silicon shield.' The figure underscores the catastrophic economic consequences any Taiwan conflict would have for global technology supply chains.

events.title: Defense Analysts: 'Justice Mission-2025' Was Full Blockade Rehearsal
events.detail: In January 2026, CSIS, RAND, and the Global Taiwan Institute publish detailed analyses concluding that the December 2025 'Justice Mission-2025' exercise constituted a comprehensive rehearsal of a legal/gray-zone blockade of Taiwan. The exercises tested coast guard enforcement in the contiguous zone (12–24 nm), naval interdiction in the EEZ, and air superiority suppression — all components of a blockade that stops short of formal declaration of war. Analysts warn this represents a strategic shift from 'punitive exercises' to 'rehearsed operations.'

media.source: Wikipedia / 2012 Unity Day parade rehearsal bombing

events.title: Taiwan Accelerates Asymmetric 'Porcupine Strategy' Defense Buildup
events.detail: In early 2026, Taiwan's MND announces accelerated procurement under its asymmetric 'porcupine strategy': rapid expansion of mobile Harpoon coastal defense cruise missile batteries, High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems (HIMARS) deployment, domestically produced Hsiung Feng III anti-ship missiles, and large-scale drone acquisition. Taiwan also extends mandatory military service from 4 months to 1 year for cohorts entering service from January 2024 onward. Reserve force combat training is significantly intensified.

events.title: PLA 2027 Centenary Readiness Milestone Approaches; Intelligence Community Alert
events.detail: As 2026 progresses, the PLA's stated goal of achieving full modernization by 2027 — the centenary of the People's Liberation Army — becomes a critical intelligence focus. US DoD officials and multiple think tanks assess the PLA is on track to have the capability (though not necessarily the will) to conduct a blockade or limited military action against Taiwan by 2027. Admiral John Aquilino (INDOPACOM commander) previously testified he believed conflict was possible 'within the next six years' — a window that now encompasses 2026–2027.

sources.name: US DoD Annual China Military Power Report

events.title: Tensions Remain Elevated at Record Levels in Early 2026
events.detail: As of March 2026, Taiwan Strait tensions remain at historically elevated levels. PLA median-line crossings continue on near-daily basis, maintaining the 'new normal' established after August 2022. Taiwan's defense budget increases, US arms deliveries accelerate, and regional allies — particularly Japan and the Philippines — deepen security coordination with Washington in what analysts describe as an 'informal security architecture' around the Taiwan Strait. No kinetic conflict has erupted, but the frequency and sophistication of PLA exercises signal continued escalation of pressure.
